critical.mandate: Story must be in ready-for-dev or in-progress status before starting. Three modes: FRESH (ready-for-dev → new implementation), REWORK (in-progress with failed reviews → fix review issues), RESUME (in-progress with checkpoint or continuing interrupted work)
Follow TDD cycle: red, green, refactor

TDD phases MUST be executed as separate, sequential steps — NEVER combine them. Do NOT write implementation code during the Red phase. Do NOT skip the Refactor phase. Each phase must produce its own checkpoint and wait for user confirmation before the next phase begins.

Write an intermediate checkpoint to {checkpoint_path}/dev-story-{story_key}.yaml after EACH TDD phase completes (Steps 6, 7, 8). Each checkpoint must include: workflow name, current step number, phase status (red_complete/green_complete/refactor_complete), key variables (story_key, epic_key, risk_level), output file path, and files_touched with sha256 checksums (shasum -a 256) for every file created or modified in that phase.
Definition of Done checklist MUST be written to the story file and every item verified before marking complete. The DoD gate (Step 10) auto-fixes failing items up to 3 iterations before halting.
All tests must pass before marking complete
When reading or running application source code, use {project-path} as the base directory — NOT {project-root}. {project-path} is resolved by the engine from global.yaml.

step.title: Load Story File
step.action: Read story .md file, parse YAML frontmatter
Extract: key, status, acceptance criteria, subtasks, dependencies
Resolve story file path: search {implementation_artifacts}/ for {story_key}-*.md. If no match, try {story_key}.md. Use the resolved path for all subsequent reads and writes.

step.title: Pre-Start Gate and Mode Detection
step.action: Verify story status is 'ready-for-dev' or 'in-progress'
step.check: HALT: Story must be in ready-for-dev or in-progress status
step.action: Detect execution mode based on story state: If status is 'ready-for-dev': set mode=FRESH — new implementation from scratch If status is 'in-progress': determine sub-mode: 1. Read story's Review Gate table (if the section exists in the story file) 2. If any Review Gate row shows FAILED → set mode=REWORK — review feedback needs to be addressed Display: "Mode: REWORK — review feedback detected. Will focus on fixing review issues." 3. Else check for checkpoint at {checkpoint_path}/dev-story-{story_key}.yaml If checkpoint exists → set mode=RESUME — interrupted work Display: "Mode: RESUME — checkpoint found. Will pick up from last phase." 4. Else → set mode=RESUME — in-progress but no checkpoint, continue from current state Display: "Mode: RESUME — continuing from current state."
Read story frontmatter: extract risk_level field (default: low if absent)
Extract epic_key from story_key — e.g., if story_key is "E1-S1", epic_key is "E1" (everything before the last hyphen-and-story-number)
step.check: Search for ATDD file using BOTH patterns (epic-level first, then story-level): 1. Epic-level: {test_artifacts}/atdd-{epic_key}*.md (e.g., atdd-E1.md) 2. Story-level: {test_artifacts}/atdd-{story_key}*.md (e.g., atdd-E1-S1.md) If EITHER pattern matches a file, the gate PASSES — log which file was found. If NEITHER pattern matches: HALT — This story is marked high-risk. No ATDD file found (searched for atdd-{epic_key}*.md and atdd-{story_key}*.md). Run /gaia-atdd before implementation.

step.title: Check for WIP
step.action: Look for checkpoint file at _memory/checkpoints/dev-story-{story_key}.yaml
step.check: Validate checksums (shasum -a 256) for each entry in files_touched — flag DELETED or MODIFIED files, offer Proceed / Start fresh / Review before resuming
Offer to resume from last checkpoint or start fresh

step.title: Update Status
step.action: Skip status change — story is already in-progress.

step.title: Figma Design Consumption
step.action: Check for figma: metadata block in story file YAML frontmatter. Expected fields: file_key (string — Figma file identifier), pages (list of page names to extract from), node_ids (list of specific node IDs), design_version (string — Figma lastModified hash for traceability). Also check ux-design.md for a figma: metadata block.

action.if: no figma: metadata block found in story file or ux-design.md

step.action: No Figma metadata present — skip all Figma-related actions. Use ux-design.md text as-is with zero behavioral change. Proceed to Step 5.
JIT-load figma-integration.md tokens and components sections for MCP extraction.

Check for cached responses in {project-path}/.figma-cache/ using composite cache key {file_key}:{page_id}:{design_version_hash}. If cache hit with valid version hash, use cached data. If cache miss or design version changed since last implementation, proceed with MCP extraction.

action.if: figma: metadata block is present and MCP available

step.action: Extract design tokens via MCP and write design-tokens.json to {planning_artifacts}/design-system/ in W3C DTCG format. Extract component specs via MCP and write component-specs.yaml to the same directory.

action.if: figma: metadata block is present and MCP unreachable

step.action: If cached data exists in {project-path}/.figma-cache/ (even with expired TTL), continue with last-known-good files and display [OFFLINE] warning: "Design data loaded from cache — MCP unreachable. Token data may be stale." Do not halt — proceed with stale data.

Compare design_version in story figma: metadata against the current Figma file version. If the design version has changed since the last implementation run, offer an incremental update: "Design has changed since last implementation (stored: {old_version}, current: {new_version}). Run incremental token update? [y/n]". Record the design_version consumed in the story figma: metadata for traceability.

JIT-load figma-integration.md export section. Generate stack-specific scaffolded code from intermediate files (design-tokens.json and component-specs.yaml) using the active dev agent's resolution table. Output token files and component scaffolds to {project-path} design system directory.

step.title: Plan Implementation
step.action: REWORK MODE — Focus plan on fixing review feedback: 1. Read the story's Review Gate table — identify which reviews FAILED 2. For each FAILED review, read its report file: - Code Review: {implementation_artifacts}/{story_key}-review.md - Security Review: {implementation_artifacts}/{story_key}-security-review.md - QA Tests: {test_artifacts}/{story_key}-qa-tests.md - Test Automation: {test_artifacts}/{story_key}-test-automation.md - Test Review: {test_artifacts}/{story_key}-test-review.md - Performance Review: {implementation_artifacts}/{story_key}-performance-review.md 3. Extract specific issues, REQUEST_CHANGES items, and FAILED criteria from each report 4. Generate a REWORK plan focused on: - What specific issues each failed review flagged - What code/tests need to change to address each issue - What does NOT need to change (passed reviews — leave alone) 5. Present the rework plan. Skip the full implementation plan sections below. 6. After presenting: proceed to Step 6 with rework-focused TDD.

RESUME MODE — Continue from current state: If checkpoint exists: Step 3 already handled the resume. Proceed from the checkpoint phase. If no checkpoint: re-assess current implementation state and generate a continuation plan focused on remaining work.

action.if: mode == FRESH or mode not set

step.action: Load relevant sections from {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md — identify architectural components, patterns, and ADRs that apply to this story's scope

action.if: mode == FRESH or mode not set

step.action: Load relevant sections from {planning_artifacts}/ux-design.md if available — identify UI components, interaction patterns, navigation flows, and accessibility requirements for this story

action.if: mode == FRESH or mode not set

step.action:
Generate a detailed implementation plan with the following mandatory sections:
**Context** — Why this change is being made: the user need or requirement it addresses, which acceptance criteria it satisfies, and the intended outcome. Reference the story's epic context and how this story fits into the larger feature.
**Root Cause / Analysis** (if bug fix or refactor) — What is broken or suboptimal and why. For new features, describe the current state (what exists today) vs. the target state (what will exist after implementation).
**Implementation Steps** — Numbered steps, each with: - Step title describing what it does - File path(s) to create or modify - What specifically changes in each file (new functions, modified logic, added config, etc.) - Why this change is needed (not just what, but the reasoning) - Any relevant ADR or architecture pattern being followed - Dependencies on previous steps (if any)
**Files to Modify** — Summary table with columns: File | Change | Reason - List every file that will be created, modified, or deleted - Group by: new files, modified files, deleted files
**Architecture Alignment** — Which ADRs apply to this story and how the implementation follows them. Flag any deviations from architecture with justification.
**UX Alignment** (if frontend story) — Which screens, components, and interaction patterns from ux-design.md apply. Reference specific wireframes or flow names.
**Testing Strategy** — How each acceptance criterion will be tested: - Map each AC to at least one test case - Specify test type (unit, integration, e2e) for each - Note any edge cases or boundary conditions to test - Reference ATDD file if this is a high-risk story
**Risks and Considerations** — Known risks, potential issues, or assumptions the plan depends on. Include: - API contracts that must be maintained - Database migration concerns (if applicable) - Performance implications - Security considerations - Backward compatibility
**Verification** — How to verify the implementation is correct end-to-end: - Commands to run (build, test, lint) - Manual verification steps if applicable - Expected outputs or behaviors to confirm
Present the full plan in a structured, readable format. Use markdown headers for each section and tables where appropriate.
step.ask: Here is the detailed implementation plan. Review the context, steps, files, testing strategy, and risks. Confirm to proceed, or request changes. [confirm / revise]

step.title: TDD Red Phase — Write Failing Tests

critical.mandate: RED PHASE ONLY: Write test files ONLY — do NOT write any implementation code in this step. Tests must define expected behavior and FAIL because the implementation does not exist yet.

step.action: For each subtask: write failing test(s) that define expected behavior (RED)
Run the test suite — verify all new tests FAIL
step.check: Tests should NOT pass yet — no implementation exists. If a test passes without implementation, it is vacuous and must be rewritten to actually test behavior.
step.action: Write intermediate checkpoint to {checkpoint_path}/dev-story-{story_key}.yaml with: workflow: dev-story, step: 6, phase: red_complete, story_key, epic_key, risk_level, output_file: {story_key}-tdd-progress.md, files_touched (list every test file created with its sha256 checksum via shasum -a 256)

Print a formatted summary directly in the conversation so the user can review without opening the file:
## RED Phase Complete
**Tests created:** {bulleted list of each test file: file path, test name, what acceptance criterion or behavior it validates}
**Test runner output:** ``` {paste the actual terminal output from the test runner showing all failures} ```
**Status:** All {count} new tests FAILING as expected
step.template-output: RED phase complete. Summary: test files created, test names, what each validates, confirmation all new tests fail. Include test runner output showing failures.
step.action: Val auto-validates this TDD output (per val_auto_in_yolo). If Val returns CRITICAL/WARNING findings: auto-fix them, re-invoke Val, repeat up to 3 times. HALT if unresolved. Save all findings and fixes to validator-sidecar memory.
step.ask: Red phase complete — tests written and all failing as expected. Review test output above. [proceed to Green phase / revise tests]

step.title: TDD Green Phase — Implement to Pass

critical.mandate: GREEN PHASE: Write the MINIMUM implementation code needed to make failing tests pass. Do NOT write final/polished code — that is for the Refactor phase.

step.check: HALT: Red phase template-output must be saved before starting Green phase. Complete Step 6 first.
step.action: For each subtask: implement minimum code to make failing tests pass (GREEN)
Run the test suite — verify all tests PASS
step.check: HALT: All tests must pass before proceeding. Fix failing tests or implementation.
step.action: Mark each completed subtask in story file

Conditional checks — for each that applies: — If API boundary changes: write or update contract tests (consumer/provider) — If database schema changes: create reversible migration script (UP + DOWN) — If gradual rollout needed: implement feature flag wrapping new code path

Write intermediate checkpoint to {checkpoint_path}/dev-story-{story_key}.yaml with: workflow: dev-story, step: 7, phase: green_complete, story_key, epic_key, risk_level, output_file: {story_key}-tdd-progress.md, files_touched (list every implementation file created/modified with its sha256 checksum via shasum -a 256)
step.template-output: GREEN phase complete. Summary: files created/modified, all tests passing, any contract tests or migrations created. Include test runner output showing all green.
step.action: Val auto-validates this TDD output (per val_auto_in_yolo). If Val returns CRITICAL/WARNING findings: auto-fix them, re-invoke Val, repeat up to 3 times. HALT if unresolved. Save all findings and fixes to validator-sidecar memory.
step.ask: Green phase complete — all tests passing with minimal implementation. Review implementation above. [proceed to Refactor phase / revise implementation]

step.title: TDD Refactor Phase

critical.mandate: REFACTOR PHASE: Improve code quality, extract abstractions, decompose components, improve naming. Do NOT skip this step even if the Green phase code looks acceptable.

step.check: HALT: Green phase template-output must be saved before starting Refactor phase. Complete Step 7 first.
step.action: Refactor implementation while keeping all tests green: extract shared utilities, decompose large functions, improve naming, remove duplication
Run the test suite — verify all tests still PASS after refactoring
step.check: HALT: Refactoring broke tests. Revert or fix before proceeding.
step.action: Write intermediate checkpoint to {checkpoint_path}/dev-story-{story_key}.yaml with: workflow: dev-story, step: 8, phase: refactor_complete, story_key, epic_key, risk_level, output_file: {story_key}-tdd-progress.md, files_touched (list ALL files created/modified across all 3 phases with sha256 checksums via shasum -a 256)

Print a formatted summary directly in the conversation so the user can review without opening the file:
## REFACTOR Phase Complete
**What was refactored:** {bulleted list: what changed and why — extracted utilities, decomposed functions, improved naming, removed duplication}
**Test runner output:** ``` {paste the actual terminal output from the test runner confirming all tests still pass} ```
**Status:** All {count} tests STILL PASSING after refactor
step.template-output: REFACTOR phase complete. Summary: what was refactored, why, confirmation all tests still pass. Include test runner output.
step.action: Val auto-validates this TDD output (per val_auto_in_yolo). If Val returns CRITICAL/WARNING findings: auto-fix them, re-invoke Val, repeat up to 3 times. HALT if unresolved. Save all findings and fixes to validator-sidecar memory.
step.ask: Refactor phase complete — all tests still passing. Review refactored code. [proceed / continue refactoring]

step.title: Capture Findings
step.action: Review any out-of-scope issues discovered during implementation (e.g., missing setup scripts, environment gaps, tech debt, broken tooling)
For each finding: add a row to the Findings table in the story file with type, severity, finding description, and suggested action
If no findings were discovered, leave the Findings table empty

step.title: Definition of Done Verification
step.action: Write or update the "Definition of Done" section in the story file with the following checklist items. For each item, run the verification described and mark it [x] if it passes or [ ] if it fails:
- [ ] **Code compiles** — run the project build command (e.g., npm run build, mvn compile, flutter build). Mark [x] if exit code 0, [ ] if errors.
- [ ] **All tests pass** — run the full test suite. Mark [x] if all tests pass, [ ] if any fail.
- [ ] **All acceptance criteria met** — compare each AC from the story against the implementation. Mark [x] only if every AC is demonstrably satisfied.
- [ ] **No linting/formatting errors** — run the project linter (e.g., eslint, pylint, dart analyze). Mark [x] if no errors, [ ] if errors found.
- [ ] **Code follows project conventions** — verify naming, file structure, and patterns match existing codebase. Mark [x] if consistent.
- [ ] **No hardcoded secrets or credentials** — scan new/modified files for API keys, passwords, tokens, connection strings. Mark [x] if clean.
- [ ] **All subtasks marked complete** — verify every subtask in the story file is checked off. Mark [x] if all done.
- [ ] **Documentation updated** — if the change affects APIs, configs, or user-facing behavior, verify that relevant docs are updated. Mark [x] if not applicable or if docs are updated.

PR MERGE DOD ITEM (E20-S9 / FR-251): Render a dedicated "PR merged to {promotion_chain[0].branch}" item under the Quality group of the Definition of Done. This is the ONLY DoD line where the branch name from ci_cd.promotion_chain[0].branch is interpolated at render time — the literal token string "{promotion_chain[0].branch}" MUST NEVER appear in the rendered story file. Resolution logic: 1. Read {project-root}/_gaia/_config/global.yaml and look up ci_cd.promotion_chain. Treat the chain as ABSENT when ANY of the following variants is encountered: (a) the ci_cd block is absent; (b) ci_cd is null; (c) ci_cd is an empty mapping; (d) ci_cd is present but has no promotion_chain key; (e) ci_cd.promotion_chain is null; (f) ci_cd.promotion_chain is an empty array. The chain is PRESENT only when ci_cd.promotion_chain is a non-null array with at least one entry. This matches the promotion-chain guard semantics used by Step 15 (Merge PR). 2. PRESENT branch: extract ci_cd.promotion_chain[0].branch (e.g., "develop", "staging") and render the DoD line as "- [ ] PR merged to {resolved_branch_name}". The item starts UNCHECKED and is auto-checked by Step 15 on successful merge. NFR-045 backward compatibility is preserved because this variant is only rendered when an opt-in ci_cd block exists. 3. ABSENT branch: render the DoD line as "- [x] PR merged — N/A (no promotion chain configured)". The item is pre-checked so it is non-blocking for legacy projects — no warning, no error, silently degrades to the pre-E20 behavior. This satisfies AC3 and AC6. 4. Write the resolved line into the story file's "Definition of Done" section under the Quality group — alongside "All tests pass", "No lint errors", "Code follows conventions". The resolution happens at render time (NEVER before); the literal "{promotion_chain[0].branch}" token MUST NEVER be written to the story markdown.

AUTO-FIX LOOP: After running all checks, if any item is marked [ ] (failing): 1. Identify each failing item and the specific issue causing it to fail 2. Auto-fix the issue (fix lint errors, fix failing tests, update docs, complete missed subtasks, remove hardcoded secrets, etc.) 3. Re-run ALL 8 DoD checks from the top and update the checklist in the story file 4. Repeat steps 1-3 up to a maximum of 3 fix iterations 5. If all 8 items pass (all marked [x]) at any point: auto-proceed to Step 11 — no user prompt needed 6. If items still fail after 3 iterations: HALT with a clear list of unresolved items
step.check: HALT: Could not resolve all Definition of Done items after 3 auto-fix attempts. Unresolved: {list of failing items with reasons}. Manual intervention required.

step.title: Load Skills JIT
step.action: Load git-workflow:commits section for commit conventions
Apply conventional commit format to all commits

step.title: Commit Changes
step.action: Stage all changes
Commit with conventional commit format: type(scope): description

step.title: Push Feature Branch

critical.mandate: This step implements FR-247 (dev-story push step, architecture §10.24.5). It is gated by the ci_cd.promotion_chain configuration in global.yaml for backward compatibility (NFR-045).

step.action: PROMOTION CHAIN GUARD (NFR-045 / ADR-033 / E20-S11): Read {project-root}/_gaia/_config/global.yaml and check whether the ci_cd promotion chain has been opted into. When the chain is absent the engine MUST skip Steps 13, 14, 15, and 16 silently — no error, no warning, no output, no injected defaults. The chain is treated as ABSENT when ANY of the following variants is encountered: (a) the ci_cd block is absent (the top-level ci_cd key does not exist); (b) ci_cd is null; (c) ci_cd is an empty mapping ({}); (d) ci_cd is present with other fields but has no promotion_chain key; (e) ci_cd.promotion_chain is null; (f) ci_cd.promotion_chain is an empty array ([]). The chain is treated as PRESENT — and Steps 13-16 execute normally — only when ci_cd.promotion_chain is a non-null array with at least one entry. The canonical predicate lives in {project-path}/scripts/lib/promotion-chain-guard.js (chainPresent) and is the single source of truth for every workflow that gates on the promotion chain. When the chain is absent, proceed directly to Step 17 (Post-Complete Gate). This preserves existing behavior for projects that have not opted in to the promotion chain.

NO-REMOTE DETECTION (AC2): Run `git remote -v` in {project-path}. If the output is empty (no git remote is configured), display the warning "No git remote configured — skipping push/PR/CI steps. Complete these manually." and set a skip flag to bypass Steps 14, 15, and 16. Proceed directly to Step 17.

BRANCH NAME DERIVATION (AC4): JIT-load the branch-naming section of the git-workflow skill from {project-root}/_gaia/dev/skills/git-workflow.md. Read the current checked-out branch via `git branch --show-current` in {project-path}. Validate that the current branch name follows the git-workflow skill convention (e.g., feat/{story_key}-{slug}). If the branch name does not match the convention, warn the user but continue — the current branch is authoritative.

PUSH (AC1, FR-247): Execute `git push -u origin {branch_name}` in {project-path}, where {branch_name} is the current branch resolved above. The `-u` flag sets upstream tracking, which is required for subsequent `gh pr create` in Step 14.

RETRY LOGIC (AC3): If the first push attempt fails, capture the full git stderr output. Classify the error: authentication failure (exit 128 with "Authentication failed"), network failure ("Could not resolve host", connection timeout), or other. Wait 5 seconds, then retry the push exactly once.
step.check: HALT with an actionable error message that includes the raw git error output (both attempts): "Failed to push feature branch after retry. Error classification: {auth|network|other}. Git output: {stderr}. Resolve the issue manually, then re-run /gaia-dev-story to resume from this step."
step.action: On success: report "Pushed {branch_name} to origin with upstream tracking. Ready for PR creation in Step 14."

step.title: Create Pull Request

critical.mandate: This step implements FR-248 (dev-story PR creation, architecture §10.24.5). It is gated by the ci_cd.promotion_chain configuration in global.yaml for backward compatibility (NFR-045) and by the no-remote flag set by Step 13. Step 14 targets promotion_chain[0].branch — the first environment in the chain is always the PR target (architecture rule §10.24.1).

step.action: PROMOTION CHAIN GUARD (NFR-045 / ADR-033 / E20-S11): Reuse the guard decision from Step 13 — if the ci_cd.promotion_chain was determined to be absent, skip this step silently (no error, no warning, no output) and proceed to Step 15. The canonical predicate lives in {project-path}/scripts/lib/promotion-chain-guard.js (chainPresent).
NO-REMOTE SKIP (AC4 of E20-S5): If Step 13 set the no-remote skip flag (because `git remote -v` returned empty), skip this step silently and proceed to Step 15 — PR creation requires a remote.

CI PROVIDER DETECTION (AC5, FR-248): Read the resolved global config and extract ci_cd.promotion_chain[0].ci_provider. Map the ci_provider value to the CLI tool name using the following table: — github_actions → gh — gitlab_ci → glab — other providers → consult the extensible mapping table; HALT if no mapping is registered for the provider. The first entry of promotion_chain is authoritative for PR target selection because the first environment in the chain is always the PR target (architecture §10.24.1).

TOOL AVAILABILITY CHECK (AC6): Verify the mapped CLI tool is installed by running `command -v {tool}` (or `which {tool}`) in {project-path}. If the tool is not found on PATH, HALT with the message: "Required tool {tool} not found. Install it or complete PR creation manually." Do not attempt PR creation without the tool.

EXISTING PR DETECTION (AC4, MPC-29): Before creating a new PR, query for an existing PR on the current branch targeting promotion_chain[0].branch. For github_actions: `gh pr list --head {branch_name} --base {promotion_chain[0].branch} --json number,url --jq '.[0]'` in {project-path}. For gitlab_ci: the equivalent `glab mr list` query. If a PR is found, capture its number and URL, emit the message "PR #{number} already exists — proceeding to CI check", set a pr_exists flag, and skip the creation command below.

PR TITLE CONSTRUCTION (AC2): Build the PR title using the convention `{story_key}: {story_title}` — for example, "E20-S6: Dev-Story Step 14 — Create PR". The story_key and story_title are already loaded from the story file frontmatter in Step 1.

PR BODY CONSTRUCTION (AC3): Build the PR body as a markdown block that includes (in order): (1) the story key as a heading; (2) an acceptance criteria summary — a bullet list of AC titles extracted from the story file's "## Acceptance Criteria" section; (3) a link to the story file path (relative to the repository root, e.g., `docs/implementation-artifacts/{story_key}-*.md`). The story file path provides reviewers with quick access to full context during review.

PR CREATION COMMAND (AC1, FR-248, MPC-28): If pr_exists is not set, execute the provider-specific PR creation command in {project-path}. For github_actions: `gh pr create --base {promotion_chain[0].branch} --title "{title}" --body "{body}"`. For gitlab_ci: `glab mr create --target-branch {promotion_chain[0].branch} --title "{title}" --description "{body}"`. Capture the created PR number and URL from the command output.

ERROR HANDLING: If the PR creation command fails (network failure, auth failure, permission denied, etc.), capture the full stderr output and HALT with an actionable error message that includes: (a) the exact command that failed, (b) the raw stderr, (c) a suggested retry step ("re-run /gaia-dev-story to resume from this step after resolving the issue").
On success: report "Created PR #{number} targeting {promotion_chain[0].branch}: {url}. Ready for CI check in Step 15."

step.title: Wait for CI Checks

critical.mandate: This step implements FR-249 (dev-story CI polling) and NFR-043 (15-minute default timeout) per architecture §10.24.5. It is gated by the ci_cd.promotion_chain configuration in global.yaml for backward compatibility (NFR-045). It is the primary mitigation for threat T26 (merge without passing CI) — on any path other than "all required checks passed" or "no checks configured", the step MUST halt and MUST NOT fall through to Step 16 (Merge PR).

step.action: PROMOTION CHAIN GUARD (NFR-045 / ADR-033 / E20-S11): Read {project-root}/_gaia/_config/global.yaml and check whether the ci_cd.promotion_chain has been opted into. When the chain is absent the engine MUST skip this step silently — no error, no warning, no output. The chain is treated as ABSENT when ANY of the following variants is encountered: (a) the ci_cd block is absent; (b) ci_cd is null; (c) ci_cd is an empty mapping; (d) ci_cd is present but has no promotion_chain key; (e) ci_cd.promotion_chain is null; (f) ci_cd.promotion_chain is an empty array. The chain is PRESENT only when ci_cd.promotion_chain is a non-null array with at least one entry. The canonical predicate lives in {project-path}/scripts/lib/promotion-chain-guard.js (chainPresent) and is the single source of truth — reuse the guard decision already computed by Step 13. When the chain is absent, proceed directly to Step 16.

NO-REMOTE SKIP (AC4 of E20-S5): If Step 13 set the no-remote skip flag (because `git remote -v` returned empty), skip this step silently and proceed to Step 16 — there is no PR to poll for without a remote.

NO-CHECKS AUTO-PASS (AC7): Read ci_cd.promotion_chain[0].ci_checks from the resolved global config. If the array is absent, null, or empty, emit the message "No CI checks configured — proceeding to merge" and advance directly to Step 16 without polling. This is the auto-pass fast path — no PR polling, no timeout enforcement.

TIMEOUT RESOLUTION (AC3, NFR-043) — MPC-35: Read ci_cd.ci_timeout_minutes from the resolved global.yaml. The default is 15 minutes when the field is absent. Validate that the value is a positive integer — reject non-positive or non-numeric values with a clear error: "Invalid ci_cd.ci_timeout_minutes value: {value}. Must be a positive integer (minutes)." Store the resolved timeout in minutes; conversions to seconds happen at the loop level.

PR NUMBER RECOVERY: Retrieve the {pr_number} captured by Step 14 (Create Pull Request). This is required for `gh pr checks {pr_number}`. If the PR number is missing from the workflow state (e.g., resume after context loss), re-query the remote with `gh pr list --head {branch_name} --base {promotion_chain[0].branch} --json number --jq '.[0].number'` and use the result. If no PR is found, HALT: "Cannot wait for CI — no PR number available. Re-run Step 14 or create the PR manually and resume with /gaia-resume."

CI PROVIDER → POLLING COMMAND MAPPING (AC1, FR-249): Read ci_cd.promotion_chain[0].ci_provider and map to a CLI polling command: — github_actions → `gh pr checks {pr_number} --json name,status,conclusion,startedAt,completedAt` — gitlab_ci → `glab ci status` equivalent (stub with a clear TODO if full GitLab support is out of scope for this story) — any unsupported provider → HALT with the actionable error "CI provider {ci_provider} has no supported polling command. Implement a mapping or switch providers." JSON output is preferred over `gh pr checks --watch` because --watch blocks indefinitely with no hook for progress reporting, timeouts, or termination — the workflow must control its own cadence.

POLLING LOOP — 30-SECOND CADENCE (AC1, AC2, FR-249) — MPC-25: Capture the start time using a monotonic clock (time.monotonic() in Python, process.hrtime.bigint() in Node) so elapsed-time math is immune to wall-clock skew. Enter a loop that: 1. Invokes the mapped polling command (e.g., `gh pr checks {pr_number}`) in {project-path}. 2. Parses the JSON output into a list of records: {check name, status, duration}. Status values map to pending / running / passed / failed per the state table below. Compute elapsed time as (monotonic_now - monotonic_start). 3. Renders a concise progress display containing: check name, status (pending / running / passed / failed), and elapsed time since the wait started. Use a single updating line (carriage return) in interactive TTY and line-per-poll in CI / non-interactive mode (detect via isatty(stdout)). 4. Checks terminal state (see AC5/AC6 below). If any check is in a terminal state, handle it and exit the loop. 5. Checks the elapsed timeout (see AC4 below). If exceeded, halt. 6. Sleeps 30 seconds before the next poll. At a 15-minute default timeout, the loop runs at most ~30 polls — well under the GitHub API 5000 req/hour token budget.

STATE MAPPING: GitHub Actions `conclusion` values map as follows — success / skipped / neutral → passed. failure / cancelled / timed_out / action_required → failed. Any check without a conclusion yet maps to its `status` field (queued / in_progress → pending or running). This is the authoritative terminal-state definition used by the success and failure paths below.

TRANSIENT ERROR HANDLING: A transient network error on a single poll (DNS resolution failure, connection timeout, 5xx from the CI API) MUST log a warning and retry on the next 30-second tick — do NOT halt the workflow. Only hard CLI errors (tool missing, authentication failure, 4xx auth/permission) halt with an actionable message. Count the number of consecutive transient failures; if more than 5 consecutive polls fail, treat as a hard failure and halt.

SUCCESS PATH (AC5): When all required CI checks are in a passed terminal state, exit the loop and emit a success summary containing: each check name, the per-check duration (completedAt - startedAt), and the total elapsed time. Then advance to Step 16 (Merge PR). This is the only happy path — all other paths halt.

FAILURE PATH (AC6) — MPC-26: When any required CI check is in a failed terminal state, extract the check name, a short failure log excerpt, and the CI run URL from the CLI response. Emit the failure detail, then HALT with this exact message format: "CI check {name} failed. Fix the issue, push again, and resume with /gaia-resume." Do NOT advance to Step 16. The story status remains in-progress so the developer can fix the issue and re-enter this step via /gaia-resume.

TIMEOUT PATH (AC4) — MPC-27: Before sleeping for the next poll, compare the elapsed time against the resolved timeout (in seconds). If the elapsed time meets or exceeds the timeout, build the list of checks still in pending / running state and HALT with this exact message format: "CI checks timed out after {N} minutes. Checks still pending: {list}. Resume with /gaia-resume after checks complete." Here {N} is the resolved ci_cd.ci_timeout_minutes value and {list} is a comma-separated list of pending check names. The story status MUST remain in-progress so `/gaia-resume` can re-enter Step 15 without recreating the PR. Do NOT advance to Step 16.

CHECKPOINT: After successful completion (all checks passed), write an intermediate checkpoint to {checkpoint_path}/dev-story-{story_key}.yaml capturing: step: 15, phase: ci_checks_passed, pr_number, total_elapsed_seconds, checks (list of {name, duration, conclusion}). On timeout or failure, write the checkpoint with phase: ci_timeout or phase: ci_failed so `/gaia-resume` can recover.

step.title: Merge PR

critical.mandate: This step implements FR-250 (dev-story merge-PR) per architecture §10.24.5. It is gated by the ci_cd.promotion_chain configuration in global.yaml for backward compatibility (NFR-045). It is the primary mitigation for threat T27 (force merge bypassing reviews) — it MUST NOT use the admin-override flag or any flag that bypasses branch protection, and it MUST halt on branch-protection failures with an actionable unmet-requirements list. The dev-agent's scope ends at merging to promotion_chain[0]; no further promotion (staging → prod) happens here.

step.action: PROMOTION CHAIN GUARD (NFR-045 / ADR-033 / E20-S11 / AC8): Read {project-root}/_gaia/_config/global.yaml and check whether ci_cd.promotion_chain has been opted into. When the chain is absent the engine MUST skip this step silently — no error, no warning, no output. The chain is treated as ABSENT when ANY of the following variants is encountered: (a) the ci_cd block is absent; (b) ci_cd is null; (c) ci_cd is an empty mapping; (d) ci_cd is present but has no promotion_chain key; (e) ci_cd.promotion_chain is null; (f) ci_cd.promotion_chain is an empty array. The chain is PRESENT only when ci_cd.promotion_chain is a non-null array with at least one entry. Reuse the guard decision already computed by Step 13 (Push and Create PR) if available; otherwise compute inline. When the chain is absent, proceed directly to Step 17.

MERGE STRATEGY RESOLUTION (AC1, AC9 / T27 — MPC-35): Read ci_cd.promotion_chain[0].merge_strategy from the resolved global.yaml. Validate the value is exactly one of the three allowed values: merge, squash, or rebase (case-sensitive). If the value is missing, null, or not in the allowed set, HALT BEFORE any `gh` call with this exact message: "Invalid merge_strategy '{value}' in promotion_chain[0]. Allowed: merge, squash, rebase." This is the canonical T27 mitigation — strict enum validation prevents a misconfigured merge strategy from silently bypassing review expectations.

STRATEGY FLAG MAPPING (AC2): Map the validated strategy to exactly one single gh flag: — merge → --merge — squash → --squash — rebase → --rebase Build the base command with exactly one strategy flag — NEVER combine --merge with --squash, --squash with --rebase, or any two strategy flags together. The composition code MUST produce a command containing one and only one of the three flags.

PR NUMBER RECOVERY (AC1): Retrieve the {pr_number} captured by Step 14 (Create PR) into the workflow's runtime state or checkpoint. If the PR number is missing (e.g., Step 14 was not executed, or the workflow was resumed after context loss), HALT with "No PR to merge — Step 14 output missing."

IDEMPOTENCY CHECK: Before running the merge, query `gh pr view {pr_number} --json state,mergedAt` and inspect the result. If the PR state is already `MERGED` (mergedAt is non-null), emit "PR {pr_number} already merged at {mergedAt} — skipping merge" and advance directly to Step 17. This makes Step 16 safe to re-invoke via /gaia-resume after a successful merge.

BRANCH DELETION FLAG (AC5, AC6): Read ci_cd.delete_branch_after_merge from global.yaml. Treat the field as `true` when absent or missing (default). If the value is `true`: append `--delete-branch` to the merge command. If the value is explicitly `false`: omit the flag and log "Branch deletion skipped per ci_cd.delete_branch_after_merge: false" — the remote feature branch will be preserved for post-merge inspection.

STORY-KEY COMMIT BODY (AC7): Append `--body "Story: {story_key}"` to the merge command so the merge commit body carries the story key on its own line. For squash strategy the story key MUST live in the body because GitHub rewrites the subject line. This enables `git log --grep "Story: E20-"` to surface all story commits on the target branch for traceability.

EXECUTE MERGE (AC1, AC7): Run the composed command in {project-path}: `gh pr merge {pr_number} --{strategy_flag} [--delete-branch] --body "Story: {story_key}"` Capture stdout, stderr, and exit code. The command MUST NOT contain the admin-override flag (the gh CLI accepts a flag that bypasses branch protection for repo admins — it is strictly forbidden here), `--auto`, or any other branch-protection bypass flag (T27 hard constraint). On exit code 0: emit a success summary containing the strategy used, the merge commit SHA (parsed from stdout or a follow-up `gh pr view {pr_number} --json mergeCommit`), the target branch, and whether the branch was deleted (y/n). Then advance to Step 17.

POST-MERGE STORY KEY VERIFICATION (AC7): After a successful merge, fetch the merge commit via `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/commits/{merge_sha}` (or `git log -1 --format=%B {merge_sha}` in the local clone after pulling target). Verify the body contains the line `Story: {story_key}`. If not (e.g., squash merge rewrote the message and the body was not preserved), log a warning "Story key not found in merge commit body — traceability degraded" but do NOT fail the step; the merge itself has already succeeded.

DOD AUTO-CHECK (E20-S9 / FR-251 / AC2): After a successful merge, open the story file at {implementation_artifacts}/{story_key}-*.md and locate the PR merge DoD line rendered by Step 10 under the Quality group — it reads "- [ ] PR merged to {resolved_branch}" where {resolved_branch} is the value of ci_cd.promotion_chain[0].branch. Rewrite the line in the story markdown, replacing "- [ ] PR merged to {resolved_branch}" with "- [x] PR merged to {resolved_branch}" — auto-check the DoD item in place. The N/A variant ("- [x] PR merged — N/A (no promotion chain configured)") is never rewritten because it is already pre-checked. Persist the checkpoint showing phase: dod_auto_checked alongside the merge metadata so the transition to `review` in Step 18 (when re-entered via a resumed run) can satisfy the PR merge DoD gate.

CONFLICT FAILURE PATH (AC3): Match stderr against known conflict signatures: "not mergeable", "merge conflict", "conflicts with base branch". On match, HALT with this exact message: "Merge conflict detected. Resolve conflicts locally, push, and resume with /gaia-resume." The story status MUST remain `in-progress` so the developer can resolve conflicts and re-enter the workflow via /gaia-resume. Do NOT advance to Step 17.

BRANCH PROTECTION FAILURE PATH (AC4): Match stderr against branch-protection signatures: "required status checks", "required approving reviews", "required signatures", "review required", "protected branch". On match, parse the specific unmet requirements from the stderr payload or call `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/branches/{target_branch}/protection` to enumerate the requirements. Build an actionable halt message listing each unmet requirement with the command the developer can run to satisfy it — for example: "Branch protection blocked the merge. Unmet requirements: (1) Required approvals: 1/2 — request review from @code-owners via `gh pr edit {pr_number} --add-reviewer @code-owners`; (2) Required status check 'ci/lint' missing — push a fix and re-run CI." The story status MUST remain `in-progress`. Do NOT retry with any bypass flag; do NOT use the admin-override flag under any circumstance.

GENERIC MERGE FAILURE PATH (AC3, AC4): On any other non-zero exit code that does not match conflict or branch-protection signatures, HALT with "Merge failed: {stderr_excerpt}. Resume with /gaia-resume after resolving." The story status remains `in-progress`.

CHECKPOINT: After a successful merge, write an intermediate checkpoint to {checkpoint_path}/dev-story-{story_key}.yaml capturing: step: 16, phase: merged, pr_number, merge_commit_sha, merge_strategy, target_branch, branch_deleted (boolean), story_key_in_body (boolean). On conflict, branch-protection, or generic failure, write the checkpoint with phase: merge_conflict, merge_protection_blocked, or merge_failed respectively so `/gaia-resume` can recover without recreating the PR.

step.title: Post-Complete Gate
step.action: Verify all tests pass (final confirmation)
Verify all acceptance criteria met
Verify all subtasks marked complete

Re-read the "Definition of Done" section from the story file — verify every item is checked (- [x]). Required items: code compiles, all tests pass, acceptance criteria met, no lint errors, project conventions followed, no hardcoded secrets, all subtasks complete, documentation updated.
step.check: HALT: All tests must pass before marking complete
HALT: All Definition of Done items must be checked before moving to review. Unchecked items: {list}. Return to Step 10 to resolve.

step.title: Update Status
step.action: PR MERGE DOD GATE (E20-S9 / FR-251 / AC4, AC5): Before invoking status-sync, re-read the "Definition of Done" section of the story file and locate the PR merge DoD line (the line rendered by Step 10 under the Quality group). Verify that the line is either (a) checked — "- [x] PR merged to {branch}" (auto-checked by Step 16 on successful merge), or (b) N/A — "- [x] PR merged — N/A (no promotion chain configured)" (pre-checked when ci_cd is absent). Both the checked and N/A variants are treated as GATE PASS and the workflow proceeds to status-sync. Any unchecked "- [ ] PR merged to ..." line is GATE FAIL.
step.check: HALT: Cannot transition {story_key} to `review`: DoD item 'PR merged to {branch}' is not satisfied. Re-run Step 16 (Merge PR) or resolve manually before retrying the status transition.
step.action: Append files changed list to story file

Initialize Review Gate section in story file: set all six rows (Code Review, QA Tests, Security Review, Test Automation, Test Review, Performance Review) to PENDING with report link "—". If Review Gate section already exists, reset all rows to PENDING.
step.template-output: Update story file with status change to 'review', list of files changed, and initialized Review Gate table (all 6 rows PENDING).

step.title: Auto-Run Reviews (YOLO only)
step.action: Skip this step — in normal mode, user runs /gaia-run-all-reviews manually when ready.

Spawn a subagent using the Agent tool with this prompt: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/workflows/4-implementation/run-all-reviews/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. The story key is {story_key}. Run in YOLO mode — auto-proceed past all template-outputs. Follow the workflow engine instructions EXACTLY."

When subagent returns: read the story file, parse the Review Gate table. - If ALL 6 reviews PASSED: report "All reviews passed. Story {story_key} is ready for done status." - If any review FAILED: report "{N} review(s) failed: {list of failed reviews}. Story moved to in-progress. Fix issues and re-run /gaia-run-all-reviews."
